entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hi @David_Chaviano,

I truly appreciate SAP's excellent initiative and would be delighted to participate. I’m originally from Ukraine but currently living in the Czech Republic. A few months ago, I began working in financial controlling for a company that uses SAP. I believe this opportunity would greatly enhance my skills and allow me to contribute even more productively to the company.

Thank you for considering my participation.

Best regards, Igor

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Dear @David_Chaviano ,

I am writing to confidently inform you that I have successfully completed the learning journey for the Finance Consultant - SAP S/4HANA Cloud (Public Edition) program.

 

I understand from the welcome email that completing this journey is a prerequisite for obtaining a voucher for the C_S4CFI SAP Certified Associate - Implementation Consultant - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Financial Accounting exam.

 

For your reference, I have attached screenshots documenting my completion of the learning journey. Additionally, you will find my SAP Community username within the attachment.

 

While completing the program in a short timeframe was a challenging endeavor, it has proven to be an incredibly valuable experience. I am confident that the knowledge and skills I have gained will be instrumental in both my professional development and potential career advancement.

 

Therefore, I kindly request a voucher to take the C_S4CFI exam at your earliest convenience.

 

Thank you for providing this excellent learning opportunity. I look forward to taking the next step in my SAP journey.

entry.title: Re: aATP ABC set-up not confirming orders
entry.content:
Thank you for taking your time to reply to my post.

Yes, I have set up Review Available to Check Result. Please find my Alternative determination set-up. It's a simple set-up with plant substitution (all possible plants) -full strategy and no hard constraints.

Access set-up

Results showing that delivery proposal is not possible for the above material.

I have tried with creating a SO when there is zero stock and some stocks as well. But no luck. 

But when I remove the material from the access above, delivery proposal is possible.

Note: This material-plant is not used in any other functionalities (like PAL, BOP).

entry.title: You ask, we answer - Ask Me Anything Session (Oct 24)
entry.content:
During SAP TechEd 2024, SAP unveiled significant improvements to SAP Build, which included making SAP S/4HANA extension development more efficient, introducing new generative AI features, and providing broader access to ABAP development tools. These changes empower the developer community to innovate more easily and with increased agility. 

You can read all about the announcements in Michael Ameling's blog post: Unveiling today: SAP Build meets ABAP Cloud! And the good news is: you can still access the session catalog to review the content and stay current with the latest innovations & enjoy Devtoberst sessions until October 25, check out the agenda here.

Now if you're curious about what you saw during the event and have some more questions, or you would like to dive deeper, here's a great opportunity:

We'll be hosting a live Ask Me Anything session on October 24, 3pm CET. You can watch it on YouTube, or click the preview above. In the session, the panel of experts will recap the features announced and answer your questions live. 

Can't join? No worries, drop your questions in the comments and the experts will answer them live for you to watch later 😉

Looking forward to seeing you there!

Cheers,
Maiara

entry.title: CR for Visual Studio SP36 32-bit (CRRuntime_32bit_13_0_36.msi) installs on Win11 23H2 and not on24H2
entry.content:
I have 2 Dell OptiPlex PCs that were purchased less than a year apart.  The older PC got it's hard drive wiped and factory image installed.  It ended up with Windows 11 Pro 23H2 OS build 22631.4317.
Experience Windows Feature Experience Pack 1000.22700.1041.0.

As part of my companies build we install CR for Visual Studio SP36 32-bit (CRRuntime_32bit_13_0_36.msi).  The install completed without error and reports are running fine.

The second PC just came from Dell and the image on it is Windows 11 Pro 24H2 OS build 26100.2033.  When I try to install CR for Visual Studio SP36 32-bit I get the below error.

"Error 1904. Module C:\Program Files (x86)\SAP BusinessObjects\Crystal Reports for .NET Framework 4.0\Common\SAP BusinessObjects Enterprise XI 4.0\win32_x86\crtslv.dll failed to register. HRESULT-1073741819"

I check all the .NET, Desktop Runtime and C++ Redistributable on both PCs and there are no differences.  Also, the 64bit version of SP36 installs just fine.

I'm assuming there is a change to .NET 32bit between these 2 versions of Win11 and I'm curious if anyone can confirm this.

entry.title: Re: MDG - how to validate if no entity fields data changed
entry.content: please let me know which option is working for this scenario

entry.title: Updates from 2024: Turning the keys on in an SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition system
entry.content:
The automobile industry has gone through tremendous change since its start: powerful and efficient engines, security systems, comfort features and the like. Then the key, somehow disappeared and you are able to start the engine by pressing a button. (In the meantime SAP came to life in 1972 and we have been delivering efficiencies in the whole supply chain up to point of sale by allowing companies, not only in the automotive industry, to make sense of a wealth of data generated at different data entry points.)

Starting the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition has also been undergoing some changes (it´s history is not the purpose of this post) and whilst this doesn't represent a change to new or existing customers, it's a change to experienced consultants. To new customers they don't know how it was before whilst for existing customers they have already been through these steps.

Let's start with a base to refer that in this case we're referring only to the initial systems: SAP CBC, Cloud ALM and the Starter System which bring the respective IAS tenant test/productive (for Cloud ALM). Please be reminded of the initial settings you need to have at hand and make sure you know who the IT contact - in the order form - is as she or he will receive the initial system access.

So let's now go through three important changes that have been delivered in 2024 from Product Management:

  1. Bundled provisioning
  2. Tenant information e-mail 
  3. System access e-mail

Bundled provisioning
With bundled provisioning the system requester now clicks on one system request which then opens up a wizard which goes through SAP Cloud ALM, SAP CBC and the Starter System Picture 1 - In SAP for Me you only need to do one initial system requestPicture 1 - In SAP for Me you only need to do one initial system request (starter customizing)

Picture 2 - A wizard will open up and you will go through SAP Cloud ALM, SAP CBC and Starter system

Picture 2 - A wizard will open up and you will go through SAP Cloud ALM, SAP CBC and Starter system

Tenant information e-mail
The tenant information e-mail is an initial communication which provides information on how to start the set-up for the different solutions that are included in GROW with SAP base or premium edition - according to your subscription.

Picture 3 - Tenant information e-mail

Picture 3 - Tenant information e-mail for GROW premium

System access e-mail
Once the provisioning of the system is finalized you will be able to logon and create additional users in order to get started in the system and start understanding the SAP Best Practices to prepare for the fit-to-standard workshops. The system access e-mail includes access to SAP Central Business Configuration, SAP Cloud ALM, Starter systems and SAP BTP.

Picture 4 - system access e-mail

Picture 4 - System access e-mail

To go through the user onboarding, that is the initial creation of users - after the system is available - we do cover this in the 1:1 Embedded Launch Activities, of course in the roadmap viewer your go to place for the implementation methodology you will also be able to find the detailed steps.

Now that we've gone through these changes to the initial processes join also our Onboarding Journey for the systems that you will need to request further down the implementation journey and get to know the best practices for the implementations. See you there!

entry.title: Devtoberfest 2024 Week 4 Contest Activities
entry.content:
Please note that while we are publishing contest activities on Friday, October 18th; many badge assignments won't be active in the system until Monday, October 21st.  If you happen to complete a contest activity before the badge is active, that's OK. You'll still get credit for it. It just won't show up in your gameboard totals until the new badges are activated and assigned.

If you are looking for Week 1 activities, they can be found here: Devtoberfest 2024 Week 1 Contest Activities - SAP Community

If you are looking for Week 2 activities, they can be found here: Devtoberfest 2024 Week 2 Contest Activities - SAP Community

If you are looking for Week 3 activities, they can be found here: Devtoberfest 2024 Week 3 Contest Activities - SAP Community

Welcome to Devtoberfest 2024! We are back again this year with 4 weeks of fun and learning! Starting on September 23rd you can take part in the Devtoberfest contest (CONTEST OFFICIAL RULES HERE) and collect points for tutorials, quizzes and live sessions! Collect as many points as possible until the 25th of October and qualify for prizes such a trip to next year's SAP TechEd in person experience! For general information check out the overview about taking part in the contest here: Devtoberfest Contest - SAP Community Groups 

The table below shows you the points you can earn in the second week of Devtoberfest! Every week we will post an update with the new activities that will get you points in the contest! Each activity will earn you a badge applied to your SAP Community ID and points that you can track on the “for fun only” Devtoberfest Gameboard. The scavenger hunt is also back this year! The tutorials, quizzes and fun activities to earn even more points as part of the scavenger hunt is part of week 4. And don't forget about Fun Friday Activities.  This week's Fun Friday activity will be Spooktoberfest to celebrate Halloween. 

List of activities for contest week 4: 

DAYBADGE CODECONTENTPOINTS
Monday   
Monday#63032EDevtoberfest 2024 - ABAP, Week 4, Oct. 211200
Monday#01171FPrepare the RAP-Based Travel Service200
Monday#605663Generate an SAP Fiori Elements Application based on a RAP-based Service200
Monday#30996ARefine the List Report with Annotations300
Monday#61122ERefine the Object Page with Annotations400
Monday#959595Devtoberfest 2024 - CAP, Week 4, October 211200
Monday#FFDCD3Create a Full-Stack Application with SAP Fiori UI using Joule in SAP Build Code300
Tuesday   
Tuesday#E9ECFDDevtoberfest 2024 - Tooling, Week 4, Oct. 221200
Tuesday#716B54Devtoberfest 2024 - Tooling, Watch Video on Process Automation300
Tuesday#BE9EF3Store User Preferences on Device250
Tuesday#0352A0Create Sales Order Business Process450
Tuesday#48A3BCRun the Sales Order Business Process100
Tuesday#A1A3ADCreate SAP Build App to Trigger Workflow250
Tuesday#032218Populate Dropdown with SAP Data (ES5)100
Tuesday#84BBB5Check Status of Process from SAP Build App200
Tuesday#74677BDeploy SAP Build App to SAP BTP100
Wednesday   
Wednesday#FBB496Devtoberfest 2024 - Integration, Week 4, Oct. 231200
Wednesday#B8BE83Set Up API Management from Integration Suite150
Wednesday#0B140FAdd an API Proxy to a Product150
Wednesday#E6EAEBConfigure SAP API Management policies to avoid CORS issues and generate an API Key200
Wednesday#DF57AFProtect Your API Proxy by Adding Application Key Verification200
Wednesday#35857DGet Started with API Revisions150
Wednesday#ACBFDDThe Use Case: Connectivity to Non-SAP Applications using SAP Integration Suite100
Wednesday#102529Preparing Your SAP Integration Suite Tenant250
Wednesday#2F0518Preparing Your Non-SAP Application's Trial Account150
Wednesday#AB9FC6Establish Connection Between Your BambooHR Trial and Open Connectors200
Wednesday#FDBCC7Design an Integration Flow to Connect with Open Connectors300
Wednesday#7B7898Test Your Integration Scenario100
Thursday   
Thursday#5F854CDevtoberfest 2024 - MAD (ML, AI & Data), Week 4 - Oct. 241200
Thursday#2C210BDevtoberfest 2024 - MAD (ML, AI & Data), Validation Tutorial Jump Start Session on Generative AI Hub - Oct. 24600
Thursday#70C5D3Devtoberfest 2024 - MAD (ML, AI & Data),Validation Tutorial Jump Start Session on Joule - Oct. 24600
Friday   
Friday#B9F4FCDevtoberfest 2024 - Frontend, Week 4, Oct. 251200
Friday#1F4654Get Started with an SAP Cloud Application Programming Model Project in SAP Continuous Integration and Delivery600
Friday#D4E4C0Create Automated System Tests for SAP Cloud Application Programming Model Projects600
Fun Friday   
Fun Friday Devtoberfest 2024 - Spooktoberfest1000
Scavenger Hunt   
Scavenger Hunt#9C7B48Scavenger Hunt Completion 600
Scavenger Hunt#011E1DDevtoberfest 2024 - ABAP Scavenger Hunt360
Scavenger Hunt#803668Devtoberfest 2024 - Basic CDS CLI challenge360
Scavenger Hunt#F9F9F9Devtoberfest 2024 - Formula Editor Challenge360
Scavenger Hunt#185D66Devtoberfest 2024 - Tooling, Watch Actions Video180
Scavenger Hunt#416F49Design and Deploy Your First Integration Flow480
Scavenger Hunt#548DEEExpose Integration Flow Endpoint as API and Test the Flow200
Scavenger Hunt#2A0305Use the Migration Tooling360
Scavenger Hunt#2F373DUse the Migration Assessment Application220
Scavenger Hunt#000000Enable Migration Assessment Application110
Scavenger Hunt#A1532CExecuting SAP HANA Cloud Tasks from the Command Line220
Scavenger Hunt#BDB308Authenticate to SAP HANA Cloud using X.509180
Scavenger Hunt#2E379AKey User Adaptation for SAPUI5 Applications - Introduction100
Scavenger Hunt#54285FKey User Adaptation for SAPUI5 Applications - UI Changes and Modes180
Scavenger Hunt#D1D1E0Key User Adaptation for SAPUI5 Applications - Custom Actions, Views and Embedded Content180

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hello @David_Chaviano 

I identify as a person in the below categories:

    • People in remote or underserved locations that may be not have access to the same economic opportunities as their urban or more privileged counterparts 
    • Minorities who may not have equal opportunity in their geographic location 
    • First generation college students
    • People who lack the financial means to afford a certification attempt for themselves

I am willing to learn more about SAP and to undergo the rigors of studying for SAP Certification. I have completed my first learning journey Configuring SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Core and I awaiting my certification attempt

Thank You

Mariam

entry.title: Re: SSAM Catalog Profile Selection

entry.title: ABAP BW no entries in RSBKREQUEST
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I have a program that gets the DTP Request and DTP Request Number from RSBKREQUEST using the DTP parameter or DTP from RSBKDTP. and check the table /BIC/B0007842000 but the problem is the latest record in RSBKREQUEST is all from previous years and I think it is not populating anymore. 

is there an alternatives table I can use and why the RSBKREQUEST is not populating?

entry.title: Re: Dynamically Handling OData Metadata in a Fiori Freestyle Application
entry.content:
Hi @prashanth01 ,
i know you sometimes have special requirements, but this approach is not exactly standard and can quickly backfire.
Basically, the best way is to do everything in the frontend and not use crude methods to customize it in the backend.

I noticed a few things:

  • SEGW is not the current standard. Basically, you should always prefer RAP if available.
  • “define view” is deprecated and ‘define view entity’ should always be used if available.
  • You said “developers often need to manipulate the metadata”. I absolutely disagree with that. I may add metadata in the frontend, but I never actually manipulate it.
  • The part with “sap.ui.core.format.DateFormat” should not be used in this way.
  • At the end you say “Use UI5 Smart Controls for more metadata-driven UIs.” Where is that described in the post?

entry.title: Re: SAP Datasphere: OData Connector for PowerBi
entry.content:
Hi @StephaneCottier,

To provide input parameters you should not append $metadata as part of the URL. 

eg: https://<MY_TENANT>/dwaas-core/odata/v4/consumption/relational/MY_SPACE/MY_VIEW_WITH_INPUT/MY_VIEW_WITH_INPUT(I_DATE='20200101')/Set

Is the PowerBI connector generating this URL? What are the step you are doing to make it? The PowerBI connector should support parameters when you enter full URL on the connector screen.

Best Regards
Gustavo Kath

entry.title: Re: SAP Service and Asset Manager - CICO Feature
entry.content: I am not posting any solution but adding one more check, because even we have activated this function and we are facing a issue when we do CLOCKIN, as soon as we clock in the operation is getting disappeared from list in SSAM, when we checked for the reason we found that relevant Operations work center is being removed in the back end system, please test this also , even we are raising OSS anyway

entry.title: CAP typescript: how do I get draft data from draft enabled entity?
entry.content:
Hello,

I'm using CAP with typescript and I have defined a draft-enabled entity SystemKinds. Cds-Typer is installed and Typescript types will be generated and updated each time I change my entity definition.

If I send a GET request with SystemKinds , I can see all properties of entity SystemKinds plus the draft properties IsActiveEntity, HasActiveEntity,HasDraftEntity. On sqlite database, I also see the auto-generated tables AdminConfigServices_SystemKinds_drafts and the DRAFT_DraftAdministrativeData with its properties e.g. InProcessByUser. Both tables are linked via DraftUUID.

Now, I like to display InProcessByUser in Fiori Elements list report and the date of last modification. Therefore, I try to extend my entity with these two properties. I don't know how to get access to these properties inside a READ handler. In other words, how do I get access to these auto-generated tables ? The approach with SystemKind.drafts does not work as documented in Fiori Support | capire (cloud.sap). Typescript does not know SystemKind.drafts or SystemKinds.drafts. It is not available in the type definition in folder @CDS-models.

Here is my AdminConfiguration.ts:

 

 

 

 

 

import cds from '@sap/cds';
import { SystemKind, SystemKinds }  from '#cds-models/AdminConfigurationService';

class AdminConfigurationService extends cds.ApplicationService {
  /** Registering custom event handlers */
  init() {

    // extract entities
    const { SystemKinds } = this.entities

   // Extend draft information
    this.after('READ', SystemKinds, async( response ) => { 
     // here, i like to extend response with the properties LastChangedByUser from DRAFT_DraftAdministrativeData
    // and HasDraftEntity from AdminConfigurationService_SystemKinds_drafts
    }
}​

 

 

 

 

Does anyone know how I can read draft-related administrative data?
Yours
Stephan

entry.title: Current Date Input control missing from dynamic text menu
entry.content:
Greetings,

I have a story that uses a custom Current date input control. In the past, I have been able to include dynamic text in my story that references this input control. I can still see this dynamic text in old stories but the option to select the Custom date input control does not appear to be available when I create a new story. Did this functionality change/move? Thank you.

SAP Analytics Cloud

Thank you so much.

entry.title: How to activate Event-Based production scenario for co-products?
entry.content:
We would like to understand which scenarios are supported for co-products in Event-Based Production Accounting.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to activate Event-Based production scenario for c...
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,


Currently, there are two kinds of co-products with different distribution methods for Event-Based:

  • Non-fixed-price co-products: Distributed using the apportionment method, in which the apportionment is the equivalence or percentage rule maintained on order header.

  • Fixed-price co-products: Distributed using the net-realizable-value method, in which a fixed amount is determined by multiplying the material price by quantities on BOM.


Kindly refer to the below SAP Help Portal documentation for details and examples:

Event-Based Cost Distribution (Co- and By-Products)

Joint Production


In case you need this business requirement in a different context (for example ETO), you can raise a feature request, as explained in KBA 2963059 - How to Submit Feature Requests for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Product


Best Regards,
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Questions Regarding to Deprecation of Legacy Learning Home Page
entry.content: I am also in interesting in this. Is there any reasons?

entry.title: Legacy Learning page - Deprecation and Adoption of New Learning experience timeline
entry.content:
Dear SAP Product Management,

we do not want to switch to the new Learning experience & Home Page for several reason. We want to stick still for a bit of time to the old learning page.

1) Is it possible to remove the toggle that allows the users to switch to the new learning experience page?

2) Is it possible to have only the legacy learning page, without the toggle? If yes, can you please confirm that the old learning experience will be deleted on May 2025 (with a consequent adoption of the new learning experience)?

 

Thanks,

Daniele

SAP SuccessFactors Learning

entry.title: ABAP RAP Unmanaged API not Triggering SAVE method
entry.content:
Hi Gurus,

I have created V4 API using Unmanaged RAP Scenario... When I am trying to create record using API in Gateway test tool, records are getting creating in Draft mode and they are not getting Saved. SAVE method is not getting triggered... I have Implemented Early Numbering which is getting triggered along with Create Method...

Everything is working fine when I using testing same service using Preview Option in Eclipse.

Please help what am I missing...

Early Numbering code

METHOD earlynumbering_create.

    DATA: l_tanum         TYPE /scwm/tanum,
          l_returncode(1) TYPE c.
    CALL FUNCTION 'NUMBER_GET_NEXT'
      EXPORTING
        nr_range_nr        = '01'
        object             = wmegc_nrobj_to
        subobject          = 'WD01'
      IMPORTING
        number             = l_tanum
        returncode         = l_returncode
      EXCEPTIONS
        interval_not_found = 1.
    LOOP AT entities ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<lfs_entities>).
      l_tanum = l_tanum + 1.
      mapped-warehousetask  =   VALUE #(
        FOR ls_entities IN entities WHERE ( tanum is INITIAL )
        (
            %cid    = ls_entities-%cid
            %is_draft   =   ls_entities-%is_draft
            tanum   =   l_tanum
        )
      ).
    ENDLOOP.
  ENDMETHOD.

Behavior Definition

unmanaged implementation in class zbp_i_openwt unique;
strict;
with draft;
define behavior for ZI_OPENWT alias WarehouseTask
draft table Zordim_o_d
//late numbering
early numbering
lock master
total etag ChangeAt
authorization master ( global )
etag master ChangeAt
{
  create;
  update;
  delete;
  field ( readonly ) Tanum;
  field ( readonly ) CreatedBy;
  field ( readonly ) Letyp;
  field ( readonly ) CreatedAt;
  draft action Edit;
  draft action Activate;
  draft action Discard;
  draft action Resume;
  draft determine action Prepare;
  mapping for /scwm/ordim_o {
    Lgnum   =   lgnum;
    Tanum   =   tanum;
    Letyp   = letyp;
    Procty  = procty;
    Tostat  =   tostat;
    Vlenr   =   vlenr;
    ChangeAt    =   changed_at;
    CreatedAt   =   created_at;
    CreatedBy   =   created_by;
  }
}

CREATE method

  METHOD create.
    DATA: lo_entity_store TYPE REF TO zcl_datastore_rap,
          ls_entity       TYPE /scwm/s_ordim_o_rf.
    lo_entity_store = zcl_datastore_rap=>get_object(  ).
    LOOP AT entities ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<lfs_owt>).
      ls_entity-vlenr_o   = <lfs_owt>-vlenr.
      ls_entity-nlpla_o   = <lfs_owt>-nlpla.
      INSERT VALUE #( %cid = <lfs_owt>-%cid ) INTO TABLE mapped-warehousetask.
    ENDLOOP.
    lo_entity_store->put_data( data_in = ls_entity ).
  ENDMETHOD.

Please help.

Thanks-

entry.title: Re: HANA failed to start after updating to SLES 15 SP6 fr...
entry.content: From the attachment in the note 2235581.

entry.title: Re: ABAP RAP Unmanaged API not Triggering SAVE method
entry.content:
Did you specifiy the "with unmanaged save"?

See https://help.sap.com/docs/ABAP_PLATFORM_NEW/fc4c71aa50014fd1b43721701471913d/bfc8218c53a746d8ad6bcca60d0b45a2.html

entry.title: Re: ABAP RAP Unmanaged API not Triggering SAVE method
entry.content: might want to reduce those managed tags, nothing to do with Fiori or UI5, RAP is fine by itself

entry.title: Re: SAP CPI Posting idoc (1:N) scenario
entry.content: Try Changing IDoc "Content Type" from Application/x-sap.idoc to Text/XML in IDOC connection details.

entry.title: Re: SSAM Catalog Profile Selection
entry.content: That should resolve the issue. Please debug the code and verify if the note has been implemented correctly.

entry.title: Re: SSAM Catalog Profile Selection
entry.content: It would follow the perfrence of technical objects first then use notification type. You would need to customize yourself if you are looking for a different behavior.

entry.title: Re: How to view and change the settlement profile for a p...
entry.content:
Hi @alexisme !

For ETO scenario, it is not possible to change the Settlement Receiver of the Event-Based order, kindly check the below KBA:

3490321 - Settlement Rule is not editable in the app "Display Posting Rules - Event-Based" - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

Kind Regards,
Helena

entry.title: How can I load Report from Crystal Server 2020 in my .NET application
entry.content:
Hi,

I have installed SAP Crystal Server today and uploaded a report from BI Lauch pad. I able to preview the report from BI Launch pad.

Now I want to integrate it with my existing .NET application to load the report from the above report server and render it as pdf. 

I have installed required SDK for .NET.  writing code to load the report. 

Now which physical path should I give for the report on the report server? 

Using code like below. but it is not working

string connectionString = "http://<Server name: port>/BOE/BI";

ReportDocument reportDoc = new ReportDocument();

reportDoc.Load("SampleReport", connectionString);

 

My concern is which connection string and physical path of the report should I mention?

Thanks,

Karuna

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hello! My name is Abdelrhman. I graduated last year from the commerce English section in Egypt. I’ve been studying hard and recently faced some financial challenges due to the economy. I completed an internship in a company here and was fortunate to be accepted for a job out of 20 candidates, which I’m very proud of. I’m very interested in expanding my skills in this field, and I believe this certification will be a significant help in advancing my career. Thank you for this opportunity!

Thank you @David_Chaviano

entry.title: Re: 2H2024 Learning - How does a Custom Card only show on...
entry.content: @JulieMcSwain, as I understand the situation and the current options, there isn't a way to control whether or not the custom banner/card will be displayed on the home page or not the way you described (like "Add on home page: "Yes" or "No"?") - custom pages have this option though, as you build them selecting what you'd like to be available. Their visibility on the home page is currently controlled by target groups (All Active Users, User Group IDs or Organization IDs). I'd say an enhancement request is required. I apologize if I deviated from the main concern.

entry.title: System Provisioning Improvement in SAP Activate for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
When you are starting a new implementation project, having the system provisioned early can significantly improve the success of your implementation project. In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (3-system landscape), we actively use customers experience and feedback during provisioning to refine and improve our processes, ensuring a smoother and more efficient experience for all our customers. The initial steps to access the initial systems have been improved and this blog will cover what has changed and how to find the right content to get you started toward a successful start of your implementation project. 

In the SAP Activate Methodology for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (3-system landscape) implementation roadmap, all tasks related to system provisioning can be found within the Technical Architecture & Infrastructure workstream. Here's a summary guide to navigating the system provisioning to set the stage for a successful implementation. 

1. Verifying Access 

The System Admin, also known as the Initial Admin, is responsible for ensuring that all necessary systems are requested and set up so the project team can begin the implementation project. The Initial Admin is the designated contact for receiving communications from SAP, including system credentials and upgrade information. This individual, listed as the Technical Administrator on the contract/sales order serves as the super administrator or cloud administrator. The first priority for the Initial Admin is to review the information listed in task Verify Initial Admin/Technical Administrator, this content will help ensuring you have all the authorization needed to start requesting the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Systems. 

2. Requesting Systems

Once this is done, the Initial Admin will be able to request the initial systems and tenant for SAP S/4HANA Cloud. The request is done via SAP for Me, see image below. 

One of the improvements that was recently made in this process, is the ability to request multiple tenants within a single request. SAP Cloud ALM tenant, SAP Central Business Configuration tenant and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Starter System (including Starter Customizing and Starter Development tenants) are included in the initial provisioning. More details and procedure steps can be found in task Request Initial System Access of SAP S/4HANA Cloud. 

3. Accessing Systems

To begin accessing the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (3-system landscape) initial systems for project implementation, the designated Initial Admin contact receives a consolidated email containing all the necessary access details. 

This email includes access information for the SAP Cloud ALM, SAP Central Business Configuration, SAP Business Technology Platform, and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Starter Systems, simplifying the onboarding process by bundling system links and login credentials in one communication. In the consolidated email, the initial admin will find links to each SAP Activate respective task, providing clear guidance on accessing the systems and performing the initial setup. 

4. Setting Up Systems: Getting systems ready for the project team 

Once the email is received, the initial admin proceeds to set up each system, starting with SAP Cloud ALM, then moving to the SAP Central Business Configuration tenant and Identity Authentication Service for the initial system setup. Access the SAP Business Technology Platform as guided in the email, and finally, establish the initial users for the project in the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Starter System, covering both Customizing and Development tenants. 

In the SAP Activate for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (3-system landscape), the tasks required to set up the systems can be accessed directly from the provisioning email mentioned in Part 3 above. Each task includes clear guidance to help users understand the necessary steps for system setup and how to complete them using the accelerators attached to the tasks. The image below shows examples of various accelerators that direct users to the Help Portal, where setup instructions and guidance for each system are provided. 

5. Support Channels

In the initial system provisioning, having access to the right support channels is important. The Contact Us page is an essential resource to bookmark, offering quick access to support whenever questions arise, or assistance is needed. 

  • SAP Customer Interaction Center (CIC) provides a central point of contact for queries such as existing cases and SAP for Me navigation, S-user ID and Universal ID. 
  • SAP Technical Support If customers are experiencing an issue with the functionality of their SAP solution and are unable to find a solution using the SAP Support Knowledge Search. 
  • SAP for Me Services & Support: Customers can create a support ticket using component XX-S4C-OPR-INC to ensure it reaches the right team efficiently. 

Final Remarks

System Provisioning, request, access, and setups are crucial to the early stages of any SAP S/4HANA Cloud implementation project. The Initial System Admin plays a key role in coordinating these tasks, ensuring that the project team has the right tools, processes and information in place to start the project. 

This provisioning process is continually improving, so it’s essential for the System Admin to regularly check the Roadmap Viewer for updates and changes. They can also share any questions or feedback directly in the Viewer through the feedback button.

Stay tuned for ongoing enhancements and future updates! We're always striving to improve our content, and your feedback plays a crucial role in that process. Feel free to share your thoughts and suggestions via the Roadmap Viewer page—we’d love to hear from you!

If you've made it all the way to the end of this blog, thank you! Your time and attention are truly appreciated, and we're grateful for your continued feedback.

entry.title: Create Role in Mass Tx. PFCG
entry.content:
hello, I have a question. Do you know some FM  ó  BAPI  to create Role in mass  like Tx. PFCG  ?

Thanks in advance,

entry.title: Integrating SAP CX AI Toolkit with SAP Commerce Cloud
entry.content:
Abstract

The SAP CX AI Toolkit is an advanced suite of AI-powered solutions designed to enhance the entire portfolio of SAP CX (customer experience) solutions (e.g. sales, services and commerce) by leveraging cutting-edge proprietary AI models and large language models. Especially for SAP Commerce Cloud, it offers AI-based features that allow you to e.g.:

  • extract and enrich product tags with image and text-based attributes, ensuring improved search accuracy and more personalized product recommendations,
  • generate compelling product content, ranging from captivating descriptions to engaging images,
  • enhances product discoverability through innovative visual search capabilities, making it easier for customers to find exactly what they need.

The video Using SAP CX AI Toolkit in SAP Commerce Cloud provides a brief overview of major features along with practical demonstrations. But for a full list of features supported by the SAP CX AI Toolkit, please visit the dedicated SAP CX AI Toolkit Features page and the SAP CX AI Toolkit Help Portal.

In this blog post, I will guide you through the major steps required to integrate the SAP CX AI Toolkit with SAP Commerce Cloud.

Prerequisite

You should have:

  • a provisioned SAP CX AI toolkit tenant with admin role access.
  • an SAP Commerce Cloud instance with admin role access.

Overview of the Major Integration Steps

  1. Prepare the SAP Commerce Cloud server
  2. Set up the SAP CX AI Toolkit tenant
  3. Validate the connection and synchronize the data

Let's dive into each of these steps in more detail.

Step 1: Prepare the SAP Commerce Cloud Server

1.1 Prepare the manifest.json file and build/deploy SAP Commerce Cloud

To prepare the SAP Commerce Cloud server for integration with the SAP CX AI Toolkit, you need to ensure the necessary extensions are included in the 'manifest.json' file:

 

inboundservices
integrationbackoffice
integrationbackofficetest
integrationmonitoringbackoffice
integrationservices
odata2services
odata2webservices
outboundservices
outboundsync
outboundsyncbackoffice
webhookbackoffice
webhookservices

 

Next, make sure the 'odata2webservices' service is configured in one of the nodes in the 'manifest.json' file:

 

    "aspects": [
        {
            "name": "backoffice",
            "properties": [
                ...
            ],
            "webapps": [
                ...,
                {
                    "name": "odata2webservices"
                },

 

The odata2webservices facilitate communication between SAP Commerce Cloud and the SAP CX AI Toolkit. Please ensure you know which server node will host the 'odata2webservices' service. (In my case, the backoffice node will host it for demo purposes, but typically the API node is more suitable.)

Finally, trigger a build based on the prepared 'manifest.json' file and deploy the successful build to a specific SAP Commerce Cloud environment.

1.2 Check/validate the settings of the necessary integration objects

On the successfully deployed SAP Commerce Cloud, we need to ensure the following predefined integration objects are correctly configured:

  • IntegrationService
  • ScriptService
  • OutboundChannelConfig

Navigate to the Backoffice application and switch to the Integration UI Tool. First, check if these integration objects are OAuth enabled by selecting "Integration APIs | Authentication":

If they are enabled for basic authentication, change the Authentication Type to "OAuth2" via ImpEx or manually:

Ensure all three Inbound Channel Configurations are updated:

Next, validate the exposed attributes of the integration objects. Navigate to "Integration Object Modeling | Type Modeling" and select, for example, the IntegrationService to view the exposed attributes.

Compare them with the required attributes (for later communication with the SAP CX AI Toolkit):

  • IntegrationService: authenticationType(code), integrationObject(classes, code, integrationType, items).
  • ScriptService: autodisabling, code, content, description, disabled, scriptType (code).
  • OutboundChannelConfig: autoGenerate, code, destination (additionalProperties, credentialConsumedOAuth, destinationTarget(id) , endpoint, id, url), integrationObject(id), synchronizeDelete)

E.g., the IntegrationService object should expose 2 attributes: authenticationType and the integrationObject, while the attribute authenticateType should further expose the code attribute and the attribute integrationObject should further expose the classes, code, integrationType and items attributes.

If they don't match, identify and expose the missing attributes by checking the boxes next to the required attributes in each of the three integration objects listed above.

Lastly, ensure the 'oAuthUrl' attribute is exposed as part of the ConsumedDestination attribute in the OutboundChannelConfig object using the provided ImpEx scripts below:

 

INSERT_UPDATE IntegrationObjectItemAttribute;integrationObjectItem(integrationObject(code), code)[unique = true];attributeName[unique = true];attributeDescriptor(enclosingType(code),qualifier)
;OutboundChannelConfig:ConsumedOAuthCredential;oAuthUrl;ConsumedOAuthCredential:oAuthUrl;

 

You can use the administration console (hac) to import the ImpEx scirpts.

1.3 Create the OAuthClient for authentication with CX AI Toolkit

To enable CX AI Toolkit to get the authentication and authorization key for data communication with SAP Commerce Cloud, we also need to create an OAuthClient.

First, I'll create an Employee user in the administration cockpit in Backoffice, this user will be used later to relate to the OAuthClient for access management.

I'll type in the necessary information including id, name, the default language (e.g., English) and currency (US dollar):

and ensure the user is in the admin group for demo purposes:

Next, I'll create a new OAuthClient by selecting "System | OAuth | OAuth Clients" and using the subtype "Integration Client Credentials Details":

Enter a meaningful OAuth client ID and secret (keep these for later setup on the CX AI toolkit side). Relate the newly created user to this OAuth client:

Provide the authority of ROLE_INTEGRATIONADMINGROUP and validate that the grant type is set as "client_credentials":

Enter the "basic" scope:

Set the access and refresh token validity, then finalize the creation of the OAuth client:

With these steps, the SAP Commerce Cloud server is ready for integration.

Step 2: Set Up the SAP CX AI Toolkit

I'll log into the SAP CX AI Toolkit tenant with the received admin user credentials. If you are not sure how to get the access credentials, check the getting access guide. On the home page, I'll click the user account icon on the top right, then select Settings:

In the Settings workspace, I'll click Integrations and "Add Integration":

Enter a meaningful name and choose "SAP Commerce Cloud" as the integration type:

Now fill in the additional fields:

  • Host URL: The base URL where the 'odata2webservices' service is hosted (Check the 'manifest.json' file as discussed earlier. In my case, the service is hosted on the Backoffice node, so I can copy the domain URL of the Backoffice endpoint from the environment workspace on the Cloud Portal page.).
  • Client ID: The OAuth client ID created earlier.
  • Client Secret: The OAuth client secret.

Click "Test Connection" (this might take a few seconds until the successful message is displayed)

Once the connection is successful, choose the product catalogs and classification systems as the source:

Please remember to choose all the product catalogs and classification systems that you plan to use, as you won't be able to add more later. If additional product catalogs or classification systems are needed later, you'll have to delete the current integration and create a new one. In my case, I select the two sample B2C product catalogs and the default classification system.

Please remember to save the integration. Once saved, I can check the Integration list to verify that it is active.

That's how to set up the integration in the SAP CX AI Toolkit tenant. Let's move on to the next step to see how to validate the connection and synchronize the data.

Step 3: Validate the Connection and Synchronize the Data

To confirm that the necessary integration components are created in SAP Commerce Cloud, log into Backoffice and switch to the Integration UI Tool, and navigate to "Outbound Sync | Configuration | Channels":

I can observe that two outbound channels for the two selected B2C product catalogs and one outbound channel for the default classification system have been created by the SAP CX AI Toolkit. To further verify this, I can check the Consumed Destination IDs (as shown above); I can notice that they correspond to the organization ID of the SAP CX AI Toolkit. This can be found under "Settings | Organization | Organization Details." in the SAP CX AI Toolkit tenant:

Back to SAP Commerce Cloud Backoffice, under "Containers," "Streams," and "Jobs", I can also verify that the corresponding objects are created for data communication.

Lastly, let's visit "Outbound Sync | Scheduling | CronJobs" to see the created CronJobs responsible for data synchronization from SAP Commerce Cloud to SAP CX AI Toolkit:

These CronJobs are responsible for synchronizing product data with the SAP CX AI Toolkit. Depending on the number of product catalogs selected, an equivalent number of CronJobs will be created, with each CronJob corresponding to a specific product catalog. Additionally, I can observe two extra CronJobs: one related to the classification system attributes and another covering the entire classification system, including its structure, attributes and values.

By default, the CronJobs related to the classification system and its attributes start immediately after the integration is established. When these CronJobs are executed, they will also trigger the other product catalog-related CronJobs accordingly.

To validate whether the data has been partially synchronized to the SAP CX AI Toolkit tenant, go to the tenant homepage and navigate to the Settings workspace, select "Commerce | Classification Attributes." These attributes come through the first CronJob, which should complete successfully within a few minutes. Click on the drop-down box of classification classes to view all synchronized classes.

The initial synchronization process, which continues until all the CronJobs are successfully executed, may take several hours depending on the volume of data. Although it's not recommended, it is possible to trigger the product catalog CronJobs manually or on demand. To do this, simply click on the 'Run CronJob' action button for the individual CronJob.

Conclusion

I have demonstrated the key steps needed to successfully integrate SAP Commerce Cloud with the SAP CX AI Toolkit. This setup will enable seamless AI-driven enhancements to your SAP Commerce Cloud platform.

To explore further how to use the SAP CX AI Toolkit in conjunction with SAP Commerce Cloud, please refer to the User Guide on the SAP CX AI Toolkit help portal. Additionally, keep an eye out for our upcoming learning journey about the SAP CX AI Toolkit on learning.sap.com.

If you found this blog post helpful, give it a thumbs-up. Thank you for reading!

entry.title: Re: Cloud API Enablement - FAQ
entry.content:
Hi @peter_peng  

yes, the i_journalentrytp is available in Private edition too, but "the BO documentation says that the clearing functionality is only available in S/4HANA Public edition" . so, in clean core context, what is the api recomended for developing on an Abap Cloud embedded with S/4HANA Cloud private edition 2023 SP01 ?. seems there is no sense to expose a webservice (remote API) to consume for the same abap embeded.

Regards!

entry.title: Re: Hello world
entry.content: It looks like we're on the same journey - welcome aboard!

entry.title: SAP Datasphere: Cannot allocate enough memory:Allocation failed; Reason: booked memory limit reached
entry.content:
Good day,

I have installed the Finance Foundation business content on SAP Datasphere and am attempting to preview data from the GLACCOUNTLINEITEMRAWDATA object (equivalent to ACDOCA), which contains 600 million records. I am working with an analytical model that has variables to limit the data; however, I am encountering an error that states: "Cannot allocate enough memory: Allocation failed; Reason: booked memory limit reached (SAP Note 3191224)." 

I have reviewed the SAP note but am still unable to preview the data. Has anyone else experienced this issue, and how was it resolved? Thank you for your assistance.

 

SAP Datasphere SAP HANA Cloud, SAP HANA database

entry.title: Re: crystal viewer parameter loses value on navigation
entry.content: Don, thank you very much for the assiatnce, but unfortunately, the previous version that I thought it was "good" was not complete. First, I had forgotten to clear the report from memory (report.Close() report.Dispose()). Second, because the code does not go thru the entire thing (loading the report, generating the data, setting the parameters, and finally saving the report in the session), when IsPostBack is true, it causes that a second time the report is run (with a different parameter), it has the same exact data, even though the param was different. This is not a workable solution. A different parameter means the dataset will be different. All is passed programatically thru a webform. Additionally I have 2 pages, the first one collects the input (once the user enters the parameter via input in the first page . When this occurs, the flow passes it to a second page where the dataset is generated (based on the given input passed from the previous page), and report rendered. The example provided in the KBA does not have parameters. Nevertheless, when all the process to render the report is byppassed because if the IsPostBack logic, then what you get is old data. This is only evident when you ran the reportt twice with 2 different inputs. Setting EnableParameterPrompt to true is not an option, all the input is already colelcted via a web form, the report parameters are set programatically form this input. Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hi @David_Chaviano 
My name is Abdulhaffiz from Nigeria. I am a tech enthusiast with prior experiences in Data Analytics. I made a mid-life career transition into tech in 2020 and have gained efficient mastery of data analytics. My objective is to gain specialization in SAP Analytics Cloudto take advantage of the growing niche. This plan will have me combined my domain knowledge of data analytics with the SAP Analytics Cloud to provide critical insights that businesses leveraging SAP can use to expand their business. I have already started the learning journey to that prior to reading this post and I serendipitously came across this.

Have access to the digital skills initiative will do my career a world of good as it will fast track my learning process since I will now be working with the deadlines and timelines set for the Digital Skills Initiative. I am also not new to self-study as all my certifications in Data Analytics were from self-study processes. 

Thank you, looking forward to making it through to certification.

entry.title: Re: crystal viewer parameter loses value on navigation
entry.content: EnableParameterPrompt to true allows the Viewer to use a new parameter, otherwise it always uses the same one.

entry.title: Is cost element type 90 available in S/4HANA Cloud?
entry.content:
We would like to ask if the cost element type 90 (Statistical cost element for balance sheet account) is available in S/4HANA Public Cloud.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Is cost element type 90 available in S/4HANA Cloud?
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,


Cost element category 90 - Statistical cost element for balance sheet account is no longer supported. You can still use existing G/L accounts with these settings, but you can’t create new G/L accounts with cost element category 90. Please use category 01 – Primary costs/cost-reducing revenues instead. Kindly refer to the below SAP Help Portal documentation for the available Cost Element Categories:

Cost Element Categories


Best Regards,
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: ABAP RAP Unmanaged API not Triggering SAVE method
entry.content: Hi I am using unmanaged scenario, still I need to mention “with unmanaged save” ?

entry.title: Re: ABAP RAP Unmanaged API not Triggering SAVE method
entry.content:
in the payload for creation, you have to include IsActiveEntity:true
if not, it will create draft, which will not trigger "save sequence"

entry.title: Re: JSON file template for Internal catalog

entry.title: Re: JSON file template for Internal catalog

entry.title: Re: Error interno 903
entry.content: 我有遇到类似问题, 应该是后台存储过程 TN 有触发跨帐套的 Insert 或 Update 操作. 但是确没有相应帐套的 SQL 用户权限所致.

entry.title: Re: Accessing long text of SAP Master data Objects or fields in S4 Hana through a CDS View
entry.content:
Hello @SamyD 

Did you use this to extract long text via ODP to external system (like Azure data lake)?

Hello @Jelena_Perfiljeva

Long text are CLOB objects not SSTRING, so why should it get limited to 1333, Any thoughts?

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: Current Date Input control missing from dynamic text...
entry.content: I can see that in my new story. Not sure what happened on your side.

entry.title: Re: 🟡 Spooktoberfest 2024
entry.content:
I was in one of my first SAP implementations, right at the moment of the much-anticipated GO-LIVE. 😬 The nerves were on edge, but there was also a collective confidence that everything was meticulously calculated; after all, we had performed all the necessary tests. ✅

We had transported everything to production, and everything seemed to be running smoothly. 🚀 All that was left for us consultants was to provide support, and the only module left to close was SD. The users were loading invoices one by one, migrating from their old and obsolete system to SAP. Everything was going well... until that fateful day. 😱

One morning, the HR consultant approached me and said she needed me to transport an OT (Order of Transport) to PRD. Without thinking twice and wanting to be efficient, I obediently performed the transport. 🖥️👍

Two minutes later, the calm atmosphere of the office transformed into chaos. I heard frantic voices saying that the system wasn't working, errors were appearing, and data had disappeared. 😵 I remained silent, my stomach in knots.

We began to frantically review what had happened, but no one understood what was going on. After intense investigation, we discovered the chilling truth: the OT I had transported was terrifying. 👻 It had replaced all the company configuration in the FI module, wiping out the initial Finance setup. 💥

Panic took hold of me. 😨 I had only transported one OT... How could I have caused such a disaster?? 😩

Fortunately, after hours of work and configuration adjustments, we managed to solve the problem. 🛠️ Half a day of work was lost aligning the invoices that were being processed from the old system to the new one, but in the end, the system returned to normal. 😌

Since then, every time I'm asked to transport an OT, I double and triple-check what I'm transporting. 🔍 I learned that in the SAP world, even the simplest action can have unexpected consequences. ⚠️

In the end, I survived to tell the tale and continue in the SAP world... hoping it will be the first and last terrifying story... 👻🎃

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Dear @David_Chaviano 
I identify as a person in the below categories:
・Women
・People in remote or underserved locations that may be not have access to the same economic opportunities as their urban or more privileged counterparts
・People who lack the financial means to afford a certification attempt for themselves
・People looking to switch their careers mid-life (36 year-old)

I have been self-studying the FI, MM, and SD modules and I am eager to learn more about SAP. I am currently preparing for SAP Certification to pursue a career in an SAP consulting company. I feel fortunate to have discovered the Digital Skills Initiative, which offers the opportunity to take the SAP exam for free. This is a fantastic way for me to change my career path.

Thank you and best regards.
LeYuki

entry.content:

  • https://www.linkedin.com/in/abhishek-kumar01?utm_source=share&utm_campaign=share_via&utm_content=profile&utm_medium=android_app

entry.title: Re: Dynamically Handling OData Metadata in a Fiori Freestyle Application
entry.content:
Hi @Marian_Zeis,

You’re absolutely right—manipulating metadata is rarely needed. Thank you for pointing that out, and I’ve removed the part about using UI5 Smart Controls for metadata-driven UIs.

Regarding the forecast report, my intention was to display data starting from the current month (e.g., if the user opens the report in October, the report shows data from the current date onward, covering a total of 11 months). I researched and implemented this, as it felt like an uncommon but useful requirement for forecast reports.

Could you suggest how we could handle this scenario in RAP? If you can provide any guidance or resources, I’d greatly appreciate it.

entry.title: MRP Date
entry.content:
Hi all,

I am trying to run MRP in running system but I am facing a problem. The problem is that when I will launch MRP it will calculates all the previous data but I want to calculate the data from current date. for ex:- my clint work on SAP from 2021 but i want to MRP live form Nov 2024, how to do? If there is any provision please tell. 

If any customization options pl let me now.

Thanks, 

Varshesh

entry.title: Re: How to copy from actuals and budget to forecast in sa...
entry.content:
Something like this: 

 
MEMBERSET [d/Date] = BASEMEMBER([d/Date].[h/YM], %Year%)
 
IF (MONTH([d/Date]) - 6) <= 0 THEN
DATA() = RESULTLOOKUP([d/Version] = "public.Actual")
ELSE
DATA() = RESULTLOOKUP([d/Version] = "public.Budget")
ENDI

entry.title: Re: Improve Third Party Apps Data Consumption from Datasp...
entry.content:
Hi Rebeca, if the note 2737656 does not help, I hope it will :), you will need to deep dive into the SQLs.

For example, is it the first time where you have slow performance in complex SQLs? Or every time you start the very same SQL? Compilation / HANA creates exec plan - this takes time.

Variables? Or Parameters used? Variables - HANA will create exec plan for each different variable, whereas parameters only once, where the parameters are not part of the exec / hash.  

HANA Warehouse - I assume you mean BW. BW 7.5, or BW/4? Do you consume the exposed models - HCPRs or create own VDM-likes? If exposed views, check the complexity via PlanViz (what you have to do anyway...), you can copy the exposed VDM and remove all texts f.e. reducing the complexity of the exposed view self. Check just how long the exposed view loads in studio - if it takes like 15-20+ Seconds, it is complex 🙂

Also HANA Hints are good to try out. Unfolding f.e. helps a lot. 

Complex views, complex issues 🙂

Let us know the progress please. I am sure performance issues during federation will popup very often 🙂

 

Cheers

Martin

entry.title: Re: How to update sequence number /SCWM/STOP_SEQ in heade...
entry.content:
SAP has offered to update stop sequence / loading sequence manually through warehouse monitor.

For ODO, it will be stop sequence. In case we have one truck with multiple deliveries to multiple customers, we can define the stop sequence to the delivery loaded in reversal sequence).

For ODO item level, it will be loading sequence. e.g in one truck, some items might be fragile, some sturdy, some might need to unload first. In this case, you can define ODO item level loading sequence. 

However I haven't figured out how this will work with the loading WO/WTs. You may be able to debug and find out more. The methods are:

ODO - update stop sequence - /SCWM/WHRHEAD_OUT_UPD_LOAD_SEQ

ODO item - update load sequence - /SCWM/WHRITEM_OUT_UPD_LOAD_SEQ

entry.title: Re: Creating an SAP Account (SAP Universal ID) and getting started in the community
entry.content:
Good morning Mr. David Chaviano.

SAP Universal ID: Username S0025727401
This is César Arnold. I'm a System Analyst (Bachelors Degree) with 30+ years of profissional working experience with analisys, development and maintenance to build software for companies located in Brazil. I understand ABAP (ECC and BTP), already studied SAP HANA database principles, basic of SAP MM, Sybase ASE, etc but I don't have proffesional work experience with SAP.

If possible, I would like to have an opoortunity, looking to switch my careers mid-life and maybe also lack the financial means to afford a certification attempt for myself.
Thanks for your attention I'll wait for your comments.
Best regards.

entry.title: Creating custom roles for route maps in Performance Management
entry.content:
Hi all,

I have a question on performance management forms creation. Suppose, there is an employee in the organization, who is not related to the employee whose form is created. In the form, the roles which are available is manager, A-manager, second-level manager, HR etc. But this employee is not related. There is a requirement for this person to be included in the route map. How can we create a custom role?

Please answer for the two scenarios:

1. Employee central is activated 

2. Employee central is not activated

This is a question from Delloite and KPMG interview. Please let me know.

 

P.S: Is there any concept of tiled reports?

Best regard.

entry.title: Introducing Myself – Exploring Opportunities in SAP EHS
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

Greetings..!!

I am Hareshwar Reddy, with 13 years of experience in Environment, Health, and Safety (EHS) management across multiple sectors, including construction, manufacturing, power, chemicals, and training domains. I have recently transitioned into SAP EHS and am now seeking a suitable role where I can leverage my expertise to drive impactful improvements and contribute to organizational growth.

Looking forward to connecting with professionals and exploring exciting opportunities.

Thank you!

Best regards,
Hareshwar Reddy

entry.title: Re: 🟡 Spooktoberfest 2024
entry.content:
🎃 Spooky memories of SAP Web IDE 🎃

Back in the day, when everything moved slower than a spider spinning its web, SAP Web IDE was the tool we depended on—waiting for that loading screen to finish felt like an eternity!

Every Halloween, they’d throw in some spooky flair a jack-o-lantern here, a ghost there
But those loading bars? They were still creeping along like they were haunted!

Beware, brave developers... Once you entered, you might never come out!

entry.title: CRM and NetWeaver version compatibility
entry.content:
Hi All, Could anybody tell me Which version of CRM is compatible with NetWeaver 7.5 ?

Is CRM version 705 compatible with NetWeaver 7.5?

entry.title: Re: Cloud API Enablement - FAQ
entry.content:
Hi @cesar_gr ,

in the S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition the BO documentation is the same:

https://api.sap.com/bointerface/I_JOURNALENTRYTP 

This sounds strange. I would propose to open a ticket to the corresponding SAP application component to clarify what is possible and how to proceed.

Kind Regards,
Olga.

entry.title: Re: html code for email body
entry.content:
Here is the CL_BCS_CONVERT=>STRING_TO_SOLI method. there is no character limit.

DATA: lv_html       TYPE string.
DATA: gw_text_bcsy TYPE soli_tab.

lv_html = '<html><body> html body or table data'.
lv_html = lv_html && '<table border="1">'.
lv_html = lv_html && '<TR><TH>Company</TH><TH>column label1.</TH><TH>column label2</TH></TR>'.

LOOP AT it_table INTO wa_area.
lv_html  = lv_html && '<tr><td>' && wa_area-xxx && '</td><td>' && wa_area-xxx && '</td></tr>'
endloop.

lv_html  = lv_html &&'</TABLE></body><html>'.
 CALL METHOD cl_bcs_convert=>string_to_soli
            EXPORTING
              iv_string = lv_html
            RECEIVING
              et_soli   = gw_text_bcsy.
          CLEAR : lv_html.


Regards,
Dharmesh Thanki

entry.title: Re: Reverting Container to the latest published version CLEARS ALL COMPONENTS of my app
